title: FT.EXPLAINCLI
description: Returns the execution plan for a complex query

returns.description: The execution plan in CLI format
The execution plan in CLI format
The execution plan in CLI format

go-redis.signature: FTExplainCli(ctx context.Context, index string, query string)

returns.description: The execution plan in CLI format

node_redis.signature: EXPLAINCLI(index: RedisArgument, query: RedisArgument, options?: FTSearchOptions)

returns.description: Query execution plan in CLI format

nredisstack_sync.signature: RedisResult[] ExplainCli(string indexName, string query, int? dialect = null)

returns.description: Query execution plan in CLI format

nredisstack_async.signature: Task<RedisResult[]> ExplainCliAsync(string indexName, string query, int? dialect = null)

intro:
Return the execution plan for a complex query but formatted for easier reading without using `redis-cli --raw`

[Examples](#examples)

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Return the execution plan for a complex query but formatted for easier reading without using `redis-cli --raw`

[Examples](#examples)

sections.title: Required arguments
sections.content:
index is index name. You must first create the index using [`FT.CREATE`]({{< relref "commands/ft.create/" >}}).
query is query string, as if sent to FT.SEARCH`.

sections.title: Optional arguments
sections.content: DIALECT {dialect_version} is dialect version under which to execute the query. If not specified, the query executes under the default dialect version set during module initial loading or via [`FT.CONFIG SET`]({{< relref "commands/ft.config-set/" >}}) command. {{% alert title="Note" color="warning" %}} In the returned response, a `+` on a term is an indication of stemming. {{% /alert %}}

sections.title: Examples
sections.content: Return the execution plan for a complex query {{< highlight bash >}} $ redis-cli 127.0.0.1:6379> FT.EXPLAINCLI rd "(foo bar)|(hello world) @date:[100 200]|@date:[500 +inf]" 1) INTERSECT { 2) UNION { 3) INTERSECT { 4) UNION { 5) foo 6) +foo(expanded) 7) } 8) UNION { 9) bar 10) +bar(expanded) 11) } 12) } 13) INTERSECT { 14) UNION { 15) hello 16) +hello(expanded) 17) } 18) UNION { 19) world 20) +world(expanded) 21) } 22) } 23) } 24) UNION { 25) NUMERIC {100.000000 <= @date <= 200.000000} 26) NUMERIC {500.000000 <= @date <= inf} 27) } 28) } 29) {{< / highlight >}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis Cloud
Flexible & Annual | Redis Cloud
Free & Fixed | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="ft-explaincli-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Array]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays" >}}) of [bulk string replies]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings" >}}) containing the query execution plan in CLI format.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query.

-tab-sep-

* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query.
